snippet.title: Yorkshire Cricket College | Doc Series | Episode 3 - Education
snippet.description:
🎥 Yorkshire Cricket College (#YCC) | Doc series | Episode 3: #Education

🏏 Providing the best Education opportunities at Headingley Stadium

💙At Yorkshire Cricket College we offer two brilliant qualifications for students aged 16-18 years old:

• NCFE Level 3 Extended Diploma In Sport
• Level 3 BTEC In Business

Both courses are delivered exclusively at Headingley Stadium, working in partnership with education provider SCL and delivery partner Pro Coach.

The Yorkshire Cricket Foundation’s educational programmes aim to utilise the power of cricket, the inspirational environment and world class facilities at Headingley to give the participants an unforgettable educational and cricketing experience.

🔗 For more info about our award-winning college & to sign up to a taster day click on the link below...

https://yorkshirecricketfoundation.com/our-work/view/57/yorkshire-cricket-college

snippet.title: The Bob Graham Round | my thought's, tips and experiences
snippet.description:
The Bob graham round often gets described as the best days mountain running in the Lake District, I often have thought that this is a very big statement considering the amount of possibilities there are here.

However, now that i'm up and had time to absorb just how amazing yesterday was I could not agree more. Personally I felt great throughout the whole round and that was no doubt down to the amazing support team who keeping me fueled, navigating and laughing the whole way round.

In my head I always wanted about 23 hours so to walk away with 21.26 would have just seemed impossible when I started as well as walking away ( just about ) having beaten the current reverse winter record.
Thanks again to everyone involved

snippet.title: Heartbreaking News: Young & Restless star Camryn Grimes shares shocking
snippet.description:
The recent Young and The Restless news shares that Camryn Grimes drops a holiday bombshell and it's heartbreaking. It is possible to love more than one Christmas special. Want to know the full story? Do watch this video till the end to know everything.
 
Proof positive that you just never know what you’re going to find out during an interview. In an recent interview with Soaps.com’s Kristyn Burtt, the Young and the Restless Emmy award winner Camryn Grimes (who plays Mariah) and fiancé Brock Powell about Mickey Saves Christmas, the new Disney Channel special in which they voice the Clauses, the couple revealed not only that they are Christmas fanatics but true aficionados of stop-motion animation. That, of course, is one of the reasons that they were so excited to play Santa and his missus in Mickey Saves Christmas. Although that isn’t the surprise that we have got to share. That came when Grimes, who “was raised on Rankin/Bass, essentially,” revealed that her all-time favorite of the production company’s specials was The Year Without a Santa Claus, the 1974 classic that includes an unforgettable sing-off by the Heat Miser and the Snow Miser. #YR #CamrynGrimes #Christmas

snippet.title: The Bob Graham Round - An Introduction To The Running Round - Keswick The Lake District
snippet.description:
#thebobgrahamround #fellrunning #trailrunning 

The Bob Graham Round is a route than can be run Clockwise or anti clockwise from Keswick's Moot Hall and back, approximately 66 miles summiting 42 fell tops
The Round is named after a chap called Bob Graham from Keswick but there will be an episode or two dedicated to him and some of the runners who have taken on the challenge and have become members of The Bob Graham Club.

snippet.title: Samuel L Jackson Claims he Once Beat Tiger Woods by Eight Strokes at St  Andrews
snippet.description:
Written: Max Schreiber
Voice: Michael Robles
Edit By: Sergio Robles

Samuel L. Jackson once beat Tiger Woods in a match at St. Andrews?

Seems too good to be true, however, that's what Jackson claims. Though, he said it was a “complete accident."

While appearing on The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon earlier this week, Fallon asked Jackson about the rumor and the Pulp Fiction star elaborated.

"The first time I ever played St. Andrews, they paired me with [Woods] in what was the (Alfred Dunhill Championship) and I was a 16 [handicap]," Jackson said. "So they gave me my 16 shots and I shot a 78 because he said ‘follow me’ and I stayed as close to him as I could. I shot 78, which means I beat him by about eight strokes."

Still seems hard to believe, right? Well, with The Open Championship set to be played at St. Andrews in July, maybe the two should square off in a "Capital One's The Match" type of event ahead of The Open so Jackson can have a chance to silence his doubters.

###

The bite of sundown bites 
Where evenings come and go
Curtain up to curtain call

So turn 
Turn away  
Dance 
And what have you got to give

snippet.title: #13 | Simon Yearsley: Scottish Winter Climber and Alpinist
snippet.description:
Welcome to episode 13 of Paths in the Outdoors podcast, this episode we talk to Simon Yearsley. Simon has spent his entire adult life climbing, he has first ascents all over the globe.

There was a short period where he ventured into caving and this established two of the most life affirming relationships he still holds today.

Simon talks frankly about how he balances his passions and loves. And how two major events in recent years have altered his path. Included in this podcast there is a section (crudely recorded) where his lifelong friend and climbing partner, Malcolm Bass, talks at the Fort William Mountain Festival. Malcolm had a life changing stroke which he is currently recovering from.

Thanks to Simon for sitting down with me, it was a great chat and the time flew by. Simon did want me do add that Bert Jenkins won a prize at Kendal Mountain Festival, not Keswick as he mentioned in the interview.

As discussed in the podcast, there is a page to donate to the the Move Mountains for Malcolm fundraiser page here: https://www.gofundme.com/f/move-mountains-for-malcolm

Thanks to Fort William Mountain Festival for the great weekend which brought so many people together and allowed me to get this interview. The Lochaber Chamber of Commerce and Outdoor Capital of the UK were amazing that weekend allowing me to record the podcast in their office. And thanks to Paddy Cuthbertson from Little House Media for his support in helping to organise the interview.

Remember to like and subscribe our YouTube channel.

Move Mountains for Malcolm on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/move_mountains_for_malcolm/

snippet.title: #BettingPeople Interview JAMIE PIGGOTT Bloodstock Agent 1/3
snippet.description:
Bloodstock Agent Jamie Piggott, son of the legendary jockey Lester, was expected to be a jockey but instead found his forte in the world of high end bloodstock. 

In Part 1, Jamie Piggott talks about having a famous dad, sitting on a horse from an early age, a mishap in the starting stalls, a riding career that really didn’t take off but working around the world gaining experience including with Tommy Stack, Willie Haggas and Gai Waterhouse. Getting started in the world of bloodstock.

snippet.title: Michael Knighton's 'hostile' bid to follow more protests by Man Utd fans
snippet.description:
Michael Knighton's preparation of a hostile bid to remove the current owners of Manchester United Football Club is moving closer. Here is the latest from the former director who is urging supporters to reject the Glazers Fan Share Scheme because it is a road to nowhere. Here is the latest interview with filmmaker John Gubba including new footage of Sunday's protest march led by The 1958.

#GlazersOut #The1958 #MUNBRI 

Written report: https://visionsport.tv/michael-knightons-hostile-bid-to-follow-more-protests-by-manchester-united-fans/

ManUtdTheReligion is a channel created  by filmmaker John Gubba, who has been actively supporting Manchester United since the late 60s. The aim is to keep alive the legacy and history of our football club with video content across our social media network and on broadcast channels around the world.

Anyone wishing to make a donation and sponsor our future video content and broadcast documentaries can do so via https://paypal.com/paypalme/johngubba or  to discuss in person please get in touch via the email in the About section.

snippet.title: David Gibson - Seminar: Ecclesiastes - Life (4/4) Keswick Convention | Week 1
snippet.description:
Seminar: School of Biblical Studies – Life in Light of Eternity
Wisdom from the Book of Ecclesiastes for Today (Life)

If all is “meaningless, meaningless” how then should we live? What does a life well lived in light of eternity actually look like.

_________________________________________________________________________________________________

Keswick Convention 2022 | Grateful

Keswick Convention is organised by Keswick Ministries. The central vision of Keswick Ministries is to see the people of God refreshed, inspired and equipped to fulfill that calling, directed and guided by God’s Word in the power of his Spirit, for the glory of his Son. So that they Hear God’s Word, Become like God’s Son and Serve God’s mission. 

For more information, visit keswickministries.org

For more information about the books mentioned during the talks, please go to https://keswickministries.org/books-devotionals/

snippet.title: Running out of Time | How not to run the Bob Graham Round
snippet.description:
Five barely-prepared New Zealanders have a crack at running the Lake District's infamous Bob Graham Round. They know the odds are stacked against them, but do they have the grit to squeak it out anyway? 

August 2020
